Successes: Substance use programming and Narcan availability helped keep drug overdoses at a more than 10 year low. Overdose related calls to the Sheriff's Office are at a 15 year low. The Sheriff's Office and K9 teams seized large quantities of Fentanyl. Sheriff's Office staff saved lives using Narcan. Contracted with a sober living provider, a recovery and prevention response team, a peer support agency, and a mobile medication-assisted treatment unit. Implemented successful awareness and prevention programs. More people in the county jail utilized counseling and peer support. The county has improved drug take back boxes.
Challenges: Lab grown drug compounds allow illicit chemists to stay ahead of laws. Staffing shortages prevented detectives from focusing their time solely on opioid trafficking. Stagnant budgets and rising training costs prevent law enforcement from receiving up-to-date guidance on trends. A lack of affordable housing and transportation. The county could not sustain certain successful programming without grant funding. The county is seeing repeat offenders. A lack of providers have led to months-long waitlists for mental health treatment and longer waitlists for substance use treatment. Burnout among staff who work on opioid treatment and abatement efforts is high.

Spending explanations:: Adult Drug Treatment Court. The Recover Columbia County app through AppArmor.
